description: Storing screen state in an Activity/Fragment so it is lost every time the system recreates the component on rotation.
body: Failure mode: UI state lives in Activity/Fragment fields, so a configuration change (rotation, locale, theme) destroys and recreates the component and the state resets, often re-triggering loads. Detection signals: the screen resets or reloads on rotation; entered data disappears; state is held in the Activity/Fragment rather than a ViewModel. Do not store durable UI state in lifecycle components. Safer fallback: hoist state into a ViewModel that survives configuration changes, expose it as StateFlow, and use SavedStateHandle/rememberSaveable for anything that must also survive process death, then verify across rotation and process-death simulation.
